name: django-oauth2-provider

metadata.name: django-oauth2-provider
metadata.summary: Provide OAuth2 access to your app

documentation:
# django-oauth2-provider

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/caffeinehit/django-oauth2-provider.png?branch=master
```

*django-oauth2-provider* is a Django application that provides
customizable OAuth2-authentication for your Django projects.

[Documentation](http://readthedocs.org/docs/django-oauth2-provider/en/latest/)

[Help](https://groups.google.com/d/forum/django-oauth2-provider)

# License

*django-oauth2-provider* is released under the MIT License. Please see the LICENSE file for details.

functions.name: provider.utils.short_token
documentation: Generate a hash that can be used as an application identifier

functions.name: provider.utils.long_token
documentation: Generate a hash that can be used as an application secret

functions.name: provider.utils.get_token_expiry
documentation:
Return a datetime object indicating when an access token should expire.
Can be customized by setting {attr}`settings.OAUTH_EXPIRE_DELTA` to a
{attr}`datetime.timedelta` object.

functions.name: provider.utils.get_code_expiry
documentation:
Return a datetime object indicating when an authorization code should
expire.
Can be customized by setting {attr}`settings.OAUTH_EXPIRE_CODE_DELTA` to a
{attr}`datetime.timedelta` object.

functions.name: provider.utils.serialize_instance
documentation:
Since Django 1.6 items added to the session are no longer pickled,
but JSON encoded by default. We are storing partially complete models
in the session (user, account, token, ...). We cannot use standard
Django serialization, as these are models are not "complete" yet.
Serialization will start complaining about missing relations et al.

functions.name: provider.templatetags.scope.scopes
documentation:
Wrapper around {attr}`provider.scope.names` to turn an int into a list
of scope names in templates.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.backends.BaseBackend

methods.name: provider.oauth2.backends.BaseBackend.authenticate
documentation:
Override this method to implement your own authentication backend.
Return a client or `None` in case of failure.

Base backend used to authenticate clients as defined in {rfc}`1` against
our database.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.backends.BasicClientBackend
documentation:
Backend that tries to authenticate a client through HTTP authorization
headers as defined in {rfc}`2.3.1`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.backends.RequestParamsClientBackend
documentation:
Backend that tries to authenticate a client through request parameters
which might be in the request body or URI as defined in {rfc}`2.3.1`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.backends.PublicPasswordBackend
documentation:
Backend that tries to authenticate a client using username, password
and client ID. This is only available in specific circumstances:

 - grant_type is "password"
 - client.client_type is 'public'

classes.name: provider.oauth2.backends.AccessTokenBackend
documentation: Authenticate a user via access token and client object.

modules.name: provider.oauth2.urls
documentation:
The default implementation of the OAuth provider includes two public endpoints
that are meant for client (as defined in {rfc}`1`) interaction.

```
.. attribute:: ^authorize/$

    This is the URL where a client should redirect a user to for authorization.

    This endpoint expects the parameters defined in :rfc:`4.1.1` and returns
    responses as defined in :rfc:`4.1.2` and :rfc:`4.1.2.1`.
```

```
.. attribute:: ^access_token/$

    This is the URL where a client exchanges a grant for an access tokens.

    This endpoint expects different parameters depending on the grant type:

    * Access tokens: :rfc:`4.1.3`
    * Refresh tokens: :rfc:`6`
    * Password grant: :rfc:`4.3.2`

    This endpoint returns responses depending on the grant type:

    * Access tokens: :rfc:`4.1.4` and :rfc:`5.1`
    * Refresh tokens: :rfc:`4.1.4` and :rfc:`5.1`
    * Password grant: :rfc:`5.1`

    To override, remove or add grant types, override the appropriate methods on
    :class:`provider.views.AccessToken` and / or
    :class:`provider.oauth2.views.AccessTokenView`.

    Errors are outlined in :rfc:`5.2`.
```

classes.name: provider.oauth2.views.Capture
documentation: Implementation of {class}`provider.views.Capture`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.views.Authorize
documentation: Implementation of {class}`provider.views.Authorize`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.views.Redirect
documentation: Implementation of {class}`provider.views.Redirect`

classes.name: provider.oauth2.views.AccessTokenView
documentation:
Implementation of {class}`provider.views.AccessToken`.

:::{note}
This implementation does provide all default grant types defined
in {attr}`provider.views.AccessToken.grant_types`. If you
wish to disable any, you can override the {meth}`get_handler` method
*or* the {attr}`grant_types` list.
:::

modules.name: provider.sphinx
documentation: Custom Sphinx documentation module to link to parts of the OAuth2 draft.

functions.name: provider.sphinx.rfclink
documentation:
Link to the OAuth2 draft.

Returns 2 part tuple containing list of nodes to insert into the
document and a list of system messages. Both are allowed to be
empty.

```
param name

The role name used in the document.

param rawtext

The entire markup snippet, with role.

param text

The text marked with the role.

param lineno

The line number where rawtext appears in the input.

param inliner

The inliner instance that called us.

param options

Directive options for customization.

param content

The directive content for customization.
```

functions.name: provider.sphinx.setup
documentation:
Install the plugin.

```
param app

Sphinx application context.
```

modules.name: provider.oauth2.models
documentation:
Default model implementations. Custom database or OAuth backends need to
implement these models with fields and and methods to be compatible with the
views in {attr}`provider.views`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.models.Client
documentation:
Default client implementation.

Expected fields:

- {attr}`user`
- {attr}`name`
- {attr}`url`
- {attr}`redirect_url`
- {attr}`client_id`
- {attr}`client_secret`
- {attr}`client_type`

Clients are outlined in the {rfc}`2` and its subsections.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.models.Grant
documentation:
Default grant implementation. A grant is a code that can be swapped for an
access token. Grants have a limited lifetime as defined by
{attr}`provider.constants.EXPIRE_CODE_DELTA` and outlined in
{rfc}`4.1.2`

Expected fields:

- {attr}`user`
- {attr}`client` - {class}`Client`
- {attr}`code`
- {attr}`expires` - {attr}`datetime.datetime`
- {attr}`redirect_uri`
- {attr}`scope`

classes.name: provider.oauth2.models.AccessToken

methods.name: provider.oauth2.models.AccessToken.get_expire_delta
documentation: Return the number of seconds until this token expires.

Default access token implementation. An access token is a time limited
token to access a user's resources.

Access tokens are outlined {rfc}`5`.

Expected fields:

- {attr}`user`
- {attr}`token`
- {attr}`client` - {class}`Client`
- {attr}`expires` - {attr}`datetime.datetime`
- {attr}`scope`

Expected methods:

- {meth}`get_expire_delta` - returns an integer representing seconds to
  : expiry

classes.name: provider.oauth2.models.RefreshToken
documentation:
Default refresh token implementation. A refresh token can be swapped for a
new access token when said token expires.

Expected fields:

- {attr}`user`
- {attr}`token`
- {attr}`access_token` - {class}`AccessToken`
- {attr}`client` - {class}`Client`
- {attr}`expired` - `boolean`

documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

documentation: Form to create new consumers.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.ClientAuthForm
documentation:
Client authentication form. Required to make sure that we're dealing with a
real client. Form is used in {attr}`provider.oauth2.backends` to validate
the client.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.ScopeChoiceField

methods.name: provider.oauth2.forms.ScopeChoiceField.validate
documentation: Validates that the input is a list or tuple.

Custom form field that seperates values on space as defined in
{rfc}`3.3`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.ScopeMixin

methods.name: provider.oauth2.forms.ScopeMixin.clean_scope
documentation:
The scope is assembled by combining all the set flags into a single
integer value which we can later check again for set bits.

If *no* scope is set, we return the default scope which is the first
defined scope in {attr}`provider.constants.SCOPES`.
Form mixin to clean scope fields.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm

methods.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm.clean_response_type
documentation: {rfc}`3.1.1` Lists of values are space delimited.

methods.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm.clean_redirect_uri
documentation:
{rfc}`3.1.2` The redirect value has to match what was saved on the

: authorization server.

class_variables.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm.response_type
documentation: `"code"` or `"token"` depending on the grant type.

class_variables.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm.redirect_uri
documentation:
Where the client would like to redirect the user
back to. This has to match whatever value was saved while creating
the client.

class_variables.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm.state
documentation: Opaque - just pass back to the client for validation.

class_variables.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationRequestForm.scope
documentation: The scope that the authorization should include.

This form is used to validate the request data that the authorization
endpoint receives from clients.

Included data is specified in {rfc}`4.1.1`.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationForm
documentation: A form used to ask the resource owner for authorization of a given client.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.RefreshTokenGrantForm

methods.name: provider.oauth2.forms.RefreshTokenGrantForm.clean
documentation: Make sure that the scope is less or equal to the previous scope!
Checks and returns a refresh token.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationCodeGrantForm

methods.name: provider.oauth2.forms.AuthorizationCodeGrantForm.clean
documentation:
Make sure that the scope is less or equal to the scope allowed on the
grant!
Check and return an authorization grant.

classes.name: provider.oauth2.forms.PasswordGrantForm
documentation: Validate the password of a user on a password grant request.

modules.name: provider.scope
documentation:
Default scope implementation relying on bit shifting. See
{attr}`provider.constants.SCOPES` for the list of available scopes.

Scopes can be combined, such as `"read write"`. Note that a single
`"write"` scope is *not* the same as `"read write"`.

See {class}`provider.scope.to_int` on how scopes are combined.

functions.name: provider.scope.check
documentation:
Check if a desired scope `wants` is part of an available scope `has`.

Returns `False` if not, return `True` if yes.

```
example

```

If a list of scopes such as

```
READ = 1 << 1
WRITE = 1 << 2
READ_WRITE = READ | WRITE

SCOPES = (
    (READ, 'read'),
    (WRITE, 'write'),
    (READ_WRITE, 'read+write'),
)
```

is defined, we can check if a given scope is part of another:

```
>>> from provider import scope
>>> scope.check(READ, READ)
True
>>> scope.check(WRITE, READ)
False
>>> scope.check(WRITE, WRITE)
True
>>> scope.check(READ, WRITE)
False
>>> scope.check(READ, READ_WRITE)
True
>>> scope.check(WRITE, READ_WRITE)
True
```

functions.name: provider.scope.to_names
documentation:
Returns a list of scope names as defined in
{attr}`provider.constants.SCOPES` for a given scope integer.

> ```pycon
> >>> assert ['read', 'write'] == provider.scope.names(provider.constants.READ_WRITE)
> ```

functions.name: provider.scope.to_int
documentation:
Turns a list of scope names into an integer value.

```
>>> scope.to_int('read')
2
>>> scope.to_int('write')
6
>>> scope.to_int('read', 'write')
6
>>> scope.to_int('invalid')
0
>>> scope.to_int('invalid', default = 1)
1
```
